criterion: Control state is obvious at a glance
description: The Tree/Timeline/Table view seg-control and the separate Flatten modifier toggle sit in the same button row and share identical 'active' styling (white background, same size/shape), even though they represent two different kinds of state: a mutually-exclusive view selector vs. an independent on/off modifier.
failure_scenario: A user glancing at the control bar with Table + Flatten both highlighted white may read it as 'four buttons, two of which happen to be on' rather than realizing Flatten is a modifier that persists across view switches — they may click Flatten expecting it to just be another view mode, or not realize unflattening will return them to whichever view (Tree or Table) they last had active.
expected: Some visual separation (grouping, divider, or distinct active-state color) between the view-mode segmented control and the Flatten toggle so their different roles are legible without reading the tooltip or aria state.
actual: Both button groups use the same .seg-btn/.active and .btn.active white-highlight treatment in the same row with only whitespace separating them.
suggested_fix: Add a vertical divider or a subtle background box around the Tree/Timeline/Table group to distinguish it from the Expand/Collapse/Flatten/Refresh action group, or give Flatten's active state a different accent color than the view-mode active state.
